topic: How Energy Flows Through Food Chains
title: How sunlight becomes energy moving through ecosystems

lesson.learn:
Energy enters most ecosystems as sunlight captured by producers. Plants, algae, and some microbes turn that light into chemical energy through photosynthesis. Herbivores then eat the producers, carnivores eat the herbivores, and decomposers recycle what is left behind.

The key idea is that energy moves one way while matter keeps cycling. Food chains and food webs are really diagrams of that movement, showing how life depends on a constant input of energy from outside the system.
lesson.simpler: Plants capture sunlight first. Then animals eat plants, other animals eat those animals, and decomposers recycle the leftovers.
lesson.keyTakeaway: Energy flows through ecosystems from producers to consumers, while matter keeps getting reused.

thirdImageCredit.creator: Mitra, A., Castellani, C., Gentleman, W.C., Jónasdóttir, S.H., Flynn, K.J., Bode, A., Halsband, C., Kuhn, P., Licandro, P., Agersted, M.D. and Calbet, A.

lesson.deeperDive:
At every step, some energy is lost as heat, but nutrients keep circulating. That difference between energy flow and matter cycling is one of the most important ideas in ecology. A food web shows how species are linked, while a food chain shows one path through that web.

Because ecosystems are interconnected, changes at one level can affect many others. That is why energy flow is not just a textbook idea. It is the operating system of the living world.
lesson.funFact: Decomposers are the quiet recyclers that keep the cycle going.
references: Khan Academy - ecosystems and energy flow

people: Teachers - use food webs to show connectivity
Modelers - study transfers between trophic levels
places: Forests - energy moves through many layers
Lakes and oceans - food webs can be dense
years: 1940s - energy flow becomes a core ecology idea
2020s - ecosystem models track energy movement in detail
concepts: Producer - organism that captures energy first
Decomposer - organism that breaks down dead matter
Food web - a network of feeding links
